title: lotononis carinata
intro: Lotononis carinata, also known as the "stick bush," is a shrub-like plant native to the savannas and grasslands of southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Lotononis carinata include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and even showing promise in the treatment of certain bacterial infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be used to make teas, tonics, or ointments for topical application.
